items.headline: City Affordability Restrictions for Subsidized Housing Face Policy Review
items.summary: The Council is addressing what happens to affordable housing when project-based subsidy contracts expire or buildings face foreclosure. This directly affects long-term affordability protections for residents in subsidized units across San José.

items.headline: Storm Sewer System Getting City-Wide Master Planning Update
items.summary: The city is moving forward with a comprehensive master plan for the storm sewer system through an amended consultant agreement. This planning effort will guide major infrastructure improvements affecting drainage and flood management citywide.

items.headline: City Council reviews capital improvement projects for next fiscal year
items.summary: Mountain View is planning its capital spending for fiscal year 2026-27, which will determine funding for infrastructure, facilities, and public projects across the city. This study session outlines what physical improvements and investments residents can expect over the next year.

items.headline: City Council Proposes Changes to Tenant Protection Rules
items.summary: Sunnyvale is introducing an ordinance to amend its Residential Tenant Protections Program. This could affect rental regulations and tenant rights in the city.

items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan Moving Forward
items.summary: The City Council received a status report on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9. This plan will shape the future of San José's library branches and services for residents.

items.headline: Historic downtown building gets bonus square footage for rehab
items.summary: The city is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from a Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource and granting an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic safety upgrades and historic preservation work.

items.headline: City of Cupertino launches summer jobs program for youth
items.summary: The city presented details on its Summer Jobs program during the April 8 council meeting. This could be relevant for residents with teenagers seeking employment opportunities.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed for San Antonio Road
items.summary: A proposal would rezone two properties on San Antonio Road to build an 8-story building with 167 apartments (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This represents significant new housing density in that area of Palo Alto.
